_comment: This STG covers procedure code SU091A, which encompasses older HBP 1.0 codes S700135, S700136, S700137, S700138 for Ilio-Inguinal lymphadenectomy.
condition: Malignant conditions of male and female genitalia, and the skin requiring lymphadenectomy
clinical_indications: Carcinoma of Urethra, scrotum, and testis with scrotal invasion

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment
USG/CT/MRI / FNAC/Biopsy report confirming the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes including history, symptoms, signs, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission submitted?
Did the USG/CT/MRI report confirm the diagnosis?
Was the Clinical notes and USG/CT/MRI report submitted are suggestive for the surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes (history, symptoms, signs, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment, advice for admission) are incomplete or missing at pre-authorization.
USG/CT/MRI / FNAC/Biopsy report does not confirm the diagnosis at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes and/or USG/CT/MRI report are not suggestive of the surgery.
Detailed Indoor case papers are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Histopathology report is missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice is missing or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Ilio-Inguinal lymphadenectomy is most performed surgical procedure for several malignant conditions of male and female genitalia, and the skin.
Lymph node metastasis is the most important prognostic indicator for survival in squamous cell carcinoma of the penis.
Lymphadenectomy in penile cancer offers a chance for cure in low nodal burden disease.
Open inguinal lymphadenectomy is the gold standard treatment of metastatic inguinal lymph nodes.
Patients with low-stage nodal metastasis can achieve durable survival with surgery alone.
